name: Aditya Chola
description: Small-time warlord who became "Big Dog" of Kaveri Delta; ambushed and killed Pallava king for "street cred"; claimed descent from Sangam Cholas

name: Parantaka Chola
description: Son of Aditya; united Delta warlords; defeated Pandyas and Pallavas; tried to raid Sri Lanka; humbled by Rashtrakutas who killed his son Rajaditya

name: Rajaditya
description: Crown prince, son of Parantaka; killed by Rashtrakutas in battle

name: Aditya Karikalan
description: Chola prince, murdered by Brahmins from Chola court; Ponniyin Selvan novel set in this period

name: Rajaraja Cholan
description: Emerged from chaos; expanded Chola empire in all directions; built Thanjavur Periya Kovil (Big Temple); set up efficient revenue system

name: Rajendra Chola
description: Son of Rajaraja; expanded even further including Bengal coast and overseas; built rival temple at Gangaikondacholapuram

name: Uttama Chola
description: Began the efficient revenue extraction system that Rajaraja perfected

name: Sembiyan Mahadevi
description: Renovated nearly 20 temples

name: Krishnadevaraya
description: Vijayanagara emperor; sent Nayaka governor to Madurai when Pandyas could no longer maintain order

name: Thirumalai Nayak
description: Most famous Madurai Nayak ruler; built grand palace still standing; rebuilt large portions of Meenakshi Temple

name: Vasco da Gama
description: Portuguese explorer, first European to reach India by sea

name: Nader Shah
description: Persian invader who looted Mughal treasury; invasion hollowed out Mughal central authority
source: How Did a Joint-Stock Company..., Blog 2 (1700-1761)

name: Aurangzeb
description: Mughal emperor, defeated Deccan sultanates, appointed Nizam of Hyderabad

name: Tej Singh / Desingaraja
description: Local chieftain at Gingee Fort, fell into tax arrears, Nawab attacked the fort

name: Ramnad Sethupathi
description: Declared independence from Madurai Nayaks, emerged as regional power

name: Ramnad Sethupathi
description: Allied with British during Polygar Wars

name: Meenakshi (Queen Regent)
description: Queen Regent of Madurai Nayaks, requested aid from Chanda Sahib to quell rebellion, was betrayed

name: Chanda Sahib
description: Nephew of Nawab of Arcot, seized Madurai using fake Quran trick

name: Chanda Sahib
description: Captured by invading Marathas

name: Chanda Sahib
description: French-backed candidate for Nawab, besieged Trichy, beheaded by Manakji

name: Anwar-ud-din Khan
description: Nawab of Carnatic, sent army to discipline French

name: Anwar-ud-din Khan
description: His army shattered at Battle of Adyar by French

name: Anwar-ud-din Khan
description: Killed at Battle of Ambur

name: La Bourdonnais
description: French commander, captured Fort St. George

name: Dupleix (Joseph Francois)
description: French Governor, began interfering in local politics after witnessing military superiority

name: Robert Clive
description: First saw action during failed French attack

name: Robert Clive
description: Led famous Siege of Arcot, brilliant diversion that relieved Trichy

name: Muhammad Ali Khan Wallajah
description: Fled to Trichy Rockfort after father killed at Ambur

name: Muhammad Ali Khan Wallajah
description: Installed as Nawab of Arcot after Chanda Sahib's death

name: Muhammad Ali Khan Wallajah
description: Nominal ruler, drowning in debt to British (Arcot debts), relied on British bayonets

name: Muzaffar Jung
description: French-backed candidate for Nizam

name: Nasir Jung
description: British-backed candidate for Nizam

name: Manakji
description: General of Thanjavur Marathas, captured and beheaded Chanda Sahib

name: Haider Ali
description: Young army general on Mysore side during siege of Trichy

name: Haider Ali
description: Sultan of Mysore, raided Tamil Nadu during Anglo-Mysore Wars

name: Yusuf Khan (Marudhunayagam)
description: Served under Chanda Sahib and French, laid siege to Trichy

name: Yusuf Khan (Marudhunayagam)
description: Switched to British service, broke through enemy lines to supply besieged Trichy, refused Mysore bribe, exposed turncoat Ponniappan

name: Yusuf Khan (Marudhunayagam)
description: Became de facto governor of Madurai and far south

name: Yusuf Khan (Marudhunayagam)
description: Fought Puli Thevar at Nel-kattum-seval fort

name: Yusuf Khan (Marudhunayagam)
description: Rebelled against Nawab and Company, besieged, betrayed by internal mutiny (Marchand, Srinivasa Rao, Baba Sahib), executed

name: Ponniappan
description: In Yusuf Khan's service, offered bribe by Mysore to turn against British, exposed by Yusuf Khan

name: Mahfuz Khan
description: Elder brother of Nawab Wallajah, appointed governor of Trichy/Madurai/South

name: Mahfuz Khan
description: Poor job stabilizing region, couldn't collect revenue from Polygars, influence declined

name: Count de Lally
description: Aggressive French commander, captured Arcot

name: Count de Lally
description: Defeated by British at Battle of Wandiwash

name: Count de Lally
description: Captured when British besieged Pondicherry

name: Puli Thevar
description: Organized Tirunelveli polygars in resistance against revenue collection, fought Yusuf Khan

name: Marchand
description: Frenchman who led internal mutiny against Yusuf Khan

name: Srinivasa Rao
description: Participant in mutiny against Yusuf Khan

name: Baba Sahib
description: Participant in mutiny against Yusuf Khan

name: Tipu Sultan
description: Son of Haider Ali, continued raids on Tamil Nadu

name: Tipu Sultan
description: Killed at fall of Srirangapatnam

name: Veerapandiya Kattabomman
description: Polygar who refused to pay taxes, betrayed by Ettayapuram Polygar and Pudukottai Tondaiman Raja

name: Marudhu Brothers
description: Led Second Polygar War with escaped Kattabomman associates, caught and hanged

name: Tipu's Sons
description: Stationed at Vellore Fort, connected to Vellore Mutiny

name: Cholas Unite the Kaveri Delta
description: Aditya and Parantaka Chola unite small warlords through incentives (shared loot from raids), killing the Pallava king, and claiming Sangam-era Chola lineage

name: Cholas Defeat Pandyas and Pallavas
description: United Kaveri Delta cartel defeats exhausted Pandyas and Pallavas; Parantaka even tries to raid Sri Lanka

name: Rashtrakuta Invasion of Tamil Nadu
description: Rashtrakutas decimate Chola army, kill crown prince Rajaditya, drive Parantaka from Thanjavur, reach Rameswaram; leave inscription; keep Tondai Nadu as buffer zone

name: Fall of the Rashtrakutas
description: Warlord from northern frontier defeats Rashtrakuta king, ransacks capital; creates power vacuum south of Vindhyas; Cholas had nothing to do with it but benefited

name: Chola Peak: Plunder in All Directions
place: Gangavadi, Bengal coast, Sri Lanka, Chera territory, Indonesia, Malaysia
description: Without Rashtrakutas to stop them, Cholas plunder north (Rashtrakuta territory), northeast (Bengal coast), south (Pandya + Sri Lanka), west (Chera territory + Arabian Sea), and overseas (Indonesia/Malaysia on merchant ships)

name: Chola Revenue System Overhaul
place: Kaveri Delta, Tondai Nadu, Nadu Nadu
description: Cholas dismantle centuries-old Nadu institutions and local warlord loyalties; establish centralized revenue secretariat cutting out middlemen; farmer's 20 sacks now go directly to Chola coffers instead of split with local warlord and Nadu assembly

name: Chola Overseas Raid (Indonesia/Malaysia)
description: Chola army carried on merchant ships raids Southeast Asia; merchants of the 500 guild then harvest camphor and dominate sea routes to China; only known Indian dynasty to conduct trans-oceanic military raids

name: Construction of Thanjavur Periya Kovil
description: Rajaraja builds massive temple; Meikirti inscriptions on walls serve as power projection to distant villages

name: Later Pandya Revival
description: After period of Chola dominance, Pandyas return to power in Madurai; "Later Pandya" period; last Chola King reportedly disappeared into the forest (basis for Aayirathil Oruvan movie)

name: Madurai Sultanate
description: Delhi Sultanate forces sent south; governor declares independence; priests wall up real Siva Lingam in Meenakshi Temple and install duplicate; raiders desecrate fake (still visible as exhibit)

name: Vijayanagara Drives Out Madurai Sultanate
description: Vijayanagara army drives out Sultanate, restores Pandyas as vassals for next century

name: Nayaka Appointed as Governor of Madurai
description: Krishnadevaraya sends loyal general as Nayaka (Governor) when Pandya kings can no longer maintain order

name: Haider Ali's 222 Coin
description: Haider Ali's coins feature numeral 222 in Arabic - a cryptographic encoding of his name using Abjad system (Ha=8, Ya=10, Dal=4, Ra=200 = 222)

name: Dutch Occupy Pondicherry (and mint coins)
description: Dutch held Puducherry for 6 years and issued coins with "Pudu" in Tamil on reverse and Dutch Kali iconography on obverse

name: Vijayanagara Pagoda System Adopted by EIC
description: EIC obtained minting rights and adopted gold Pagoda (3.4g gold) = 36 silver Fanams; 1 Fanam = 80 copper Kasu

name: Introduction of Arcot Rupee
description: Madras Presidency begins minting Arcot Rupees alongside Pagodas to facilitate trade with other presidencies using Mughal Rupee system

name: Pagoda Demonetized
description: Presidency stops accepting Pagodas and Fanams, standardizes on Silver Rupees and copper Cash

name: Uniform Coinage Act
description: All presidencies adopt identical standardized coins: 1 Rupee = 16 Annas = 64 Paisa = 192 Pie

name: End of Free Coinage (Fiat Transition)
description: Coins no longer derive value from intrinsic metal content; transition to fiat currency

name: Thirai Cash Double Obverse Error
description: Hammered coin error - craftsperson used two identical deer dies instead of deer + battle axe; rare double obverse

name: Battle of Tallikota
description: Deccan sultanates defeat Vijayanagara Empire, leads to fragmentation into Nayak dynasties

name: Nayak Dynasties Emerge
description: Four independent Nayak kingdoms established across Tamil Nadu after Vijayanagara collapse

name: Deccan Sultanates Expand South
description: Golconda takes Tondai Nadu, Bijapur takes Nadu Nadu; Madurai and Thanjavur Nayaks pay tribute

name: Maratha Conquest of Gingee
description: Marathas conquer Gingee from Bijapur

name: Thanjavur Maratha Dynasty Established
description: Marathas establish dynasty in Thanjavur (Ekoji)

name: Mughal Siege of Gingee
description: Aurangzeb's forces besiege Gingee; pyrrhic victory that exhausted Mughal forces and prevented further southern expansion

name: Nawab of Carnatic Appointed
description: Mughals appoint Nawab for the Carnatic region, create Qiladar position at Gingee

name: Nayaka Grant - Fort St. George Founded
description: British receive 2-mile land grant, establish Fort St. George and Black Town (George Town)

name: Wave II British Expansion - Madras
description: Through leases and Mughal gifts, British expand around Madras

name: Ramnad Declares Independence
description: Sethupathi declares independence from Madurai Nayaks

name: Fall of Madurai to Nawab
description: Chanda Sahib seizes Madurai through betrayal of Queen Regent Meenakshi (fake Quran trick)

name: Maratha Invasion of Tamil Nadu
description: Thanjavur Marathas call reinforcements from Deccan; Marathas kill Nawab of Arcot, capture Chanda Sahib; Nizam drives them out

name: First Carnatic War / Battle of Adyar
place: Fort St. George, Adyar, Fort St. David, Pondicherry
description: French capture Fort St. George; Battle of Adyar - small French force shatters Nawab's army with field artillery. "Europeans ceased being traders with muskets." Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle returns Fort St. George to British.

name: Second Carnatic War
description: Succession war. Team France (Chanda Sahib + Muzaffar Jung) vs Team Britain (Wallajah + Nasir Jung). Battle of Ambur, Siege of Trichy, Clive's Siege of Arcot, beheading of Chanda Sahib, Mysore siege of Trichy. Treaty of Pondicherry. "European powers moved from players to masters."
sideB: British / Muhammad Ali Khan Wallajah / Nasir Jung

name: Battle of Ambur
description: French-backed forces defeat British-backed Nawab Anwar-ud-din (killed); Muhammad Ali flees to Trichy

name: Siege of Arcot (Clive)
description: Robert Clive captures Arcot as diversion, forces Chanda Sahib to pull troops from Trichy siege

name: Beheading of Chanda Sahib
description: Manakji, general of Thanjavur Marathas, captures and beheads Chanda Sahib; Wallajah becomes undisputed Nawab

name: Mysore Siege of Trichy
description: Wallajah reneges on promise to Mysore (refused to hand over Trichy); 3-year siege featuring young Haider Ali on Mysore side and Yusuf Khan on British side

name: Treaty of Pondicherry
description: Ends Second Carnatic War. French get puppet Nizam + Northern Circars. British install Wallajah. Nawab deeply in debt (Arcot debts).

name: Third Carnatic War / Battle of Wandiwash
description: Count de Lally captures Arcot; British win decisively at Wandiwash; British besiege and raze Pondicherry. French returned in 1763 limited to commercial trade only. Permanently removes French as rival.

name: Wave III British Expansion - Madras
description: Military force and treaties bring southern villages under British control

name: Yusuf Khan's Rebellion and Execution
description: Yusuf Khan rebels; Nawab and Company besiege Madurai; internal mutiny by Marchand, Srinivasa Rao, Baba Sahib; Yusuf Khan captured and executed

name: Anglo-Mysore Wars Raids on Tamil Nadu
description: Haider Ali and Tipu repeatedly raid Tamil Nadu; tax collection falls ~33%; Dindigul under Mysore since 1755

name: British Take Over Nawab's Administration
description: Fort St. George announces takeover of Nawab's country management, having become masters of Bengal

name: First Polygar War
description: Veerapandiya Kattabomman refuses taxes; betrayed by Ettayapuram Polygar and Pudukottai Tondaiman Raja

name: Fall of Srirangapatnam
description: Tipu Sultan killed; British claim to find correspondence between Tipu and Arcot Nawab

name: Second Polygar War
description: Marudhu brothers + escaped Kattabomman associates resist British; Ramnad, Pudukottai, Ettayapuram allied with British; Polygar leaders caught and hanged; Polygar system replaced by Ryotwari

name: Carnatic Treaty
description: British use Tipu-Nawab correspondence as pretext; bypass rightful Nawab, install puppet who signs treaty giving full administrative control to British

name: Vellore Mutiny
description: Tipu's sons stationed at Vellore; Sepoys banned from beards/religious marks; massacre of ~200 British soldiers; put down by different British garrison with Indian Sepoys. Similar causes to later Sepoy Mutiny.

name: Chola Empire ("Modern" Cholas)
place: Kaveri Delta to all Tamil Nadu to overseas
description: No connection to Sangam Cholas; co-opted brand name. United Kaveri Delta warlords, defeated Pandyas/Pallavas, bullied Deccan, raided Indonesia/Malaysia. Built Thanjavur Big Temple. Efficient centralized revenue system. Only Tamil dynasty to project power northward and overseas.

name: Pandyas
description: Ancient dynasty; fought Pallavas for 300 years; defeated by Cholas; Later Pandya revival ~1250; fell to Delhi Sultanate 1335
source: Cholas Part 1, Madurai Through the Ages

name: Pallavas
description: Fought Pandyas for centuries; king killed by Aditya Chola; splinter groups persisted

name: Rashtrakutas
description: "Most dangerous gang in India for 200 years"; periodically raided Tamil Nadu; crushed Cholas ~950 AD; fell to northern frontier warlord ~972 AD

name: Madurai Sultanate
description: Delhi Sultanate breakaway; desecrated temples; driven out by Vijayanagara

name: 500 Merchant Guild
description: Multinational merchant guild with own flag, private armies, code of conduct; paid customs to Cholas; possibly Cholas were their military arm; harvested camphor from Indonesia, exploited Sri Lankan pearl fisheries

name: Venad / Travancore
description: Dynasty that became Travancore; issued Thirai Cash coins with deer and battle axe

name: Vijayanagara Empire
description: Drove out Madurai Sultanate; restored Pandyas then appointed Nayaka governors; collapsed after Battle of Tallikota

name: Chandragiri Nayaks
description: Fell to Golconda influence

name: Gingee Nayaks
description: Fell to Bijapur control

name: Madurai Nayaks
description: Gradually declined; lost Ramnad 1707; fell to Nawab 1736

name: Thanjavur Nayaks
description: Replaced by Maratha dynasty

name: Golconda Sultanate
description: Expanded into Tamil Nadu, defeated by Mughals

name: Bijapur Sultanate
description: Expanded into Tamil Nadu, defeated by Mughals

name: Mughal Empire
description: Appointed Nawab of Carnatic and Nizam of Hyderabad; central power hollowed out after Nadir Shah's invasion 1739

name: Thanjavur Marathas
description: Established by Ekoji; allied with British in Carnatic Wars; Manakji beheaded Chanda Sahib; vassals of Nawab nominally

name: Nawab of Arcot / Carnatic
description: Mughal appointee to rising power to puppet of British to dissolved by Carnatic Treaty

name: Ramnad (Sethupathi)
description: Independent from 1707; allied with British by 1801

name: Mysore Kingdom
description: Haider Ali to Tipu Sultan; major threat to both Nawab and British; fell at Srirangapatnam

name: British East India Company
place: Madras/Fort St. George to all Tamil Nadu
description: Traders to military power to kingmakers to administrators to sovereign rulers

name: French East India Company
description: Dominant European power briefly; Dupleix's political interference; eliminated at Wandiwash/Pondicherry; reduced to commercial only

name: Dutch East India Company
description: Dominated 17th century European trade; acquired Nagapattanam from Portuguese; occupied Pondicherry in 1690s

name: Portuguese
description: First European presence; San Thome Factory; later displaced by British

name: Danish
description: Small trading presence

name: Polygars
place: Southern Tamil Nadu - Panchalankurichi, Sivagangai, etc.
description: Regional chieftains; resisted tax collection; destroyed by Polygar Wars and Ryotwari system

name: Pudukottai (Tondaiman Raja)
description: Allied with British, betrayed Kattabomman

name: Ettayapuram
description: Allied with British, betrayed Kattabomman

name: Sivagangai
description: Marudhu brothers; resisted British in Second Polygar War
